Children in the U.S. face food insecurity — including the student-athletes in your community.
Focus•Trust• Finish|Every athlete needs fuel. Every kid deserves it.
Our Framework

The FTF Framework™

Three pillars. One standard. Built for athletes, students, executives, and leaders at every level.

F
Focus

Know where you're going. We help athletes and organizations direct their energy toward what matters most — eliminating distraction and building clarity of purpose.

T
Trust

Believe in the process. High-performing teams are built on trust — in each other, in the system, and in the mission. With it, teams execute when it matters most.

F
Finish

Execute. Every time. The world rewards finishers — not starters. We develop people who complete what they commit to, on the field and beyond it.

FTF
Meet the Founder

Meet the Founder.

Fuel Their Future was built by a 14-year-old who saw a gap no one was closing — and decided to close it himself.

Founder
Lennon Fells

At 14, Lennon has already raised $13,000 for hunger relief, launched a business donating to families in need, and founded Fuel Their Future — driven by the belief that no young athlete should go without the nutrition they need to compete.

"At 14, he's already proving that the next generation of leaders doesn't wait to be asked."

What We Offer

Speaking Engagements

High-impact, one-time speaking engagements for schools, sports organizations, and corporations — powered by a decade of NFL experience and a mission that matters.

01
🏫
School Assemblies & K–12 Events

Daniel speaks directly to students about performance, nutrition, and the responsibility to serve their communities. High-energy and impactful — built to move students from inspiration to action.

  • Focus, Trust, Finish — the student-athlete mindset
  • Why nutrition is your #1 performance tool
  • Community leadership and giving back
  • Resilience through adversity
02
🏈
Sports Teams & Athletic Organizations

From youth leagues to college programs, Daniel delivers the inside story of what it takes to compete at the highest level — and how nutrition, teamwork, and mental toughness create champions.

  • Building a winning team culture
  • Elite nutrition for performance
  • Mental preparation and pressure management
  • The path from youth sports to the pros
03
🏢
Corporate Keynotes & Leadership Events

What the NFL taught Daniel about accountability, trust, and execution translates directly to the boardroom. A powerful keynote that leaves every leader rethinking what it means to perform and serve.

  • Building a winning culture — the NFL playbook
  • Accountability and execution under pressure
  • Purpose-driven leadership
  • Fuel to Win — performance and equity

Fuel Their Future — Our Founder

Lennon
Fells

At 14, he's already changing communities. The young visionary who saw a gap no one was closing — and decided to close it himself.

Focus•Trust• Finish|Every athlete needs fuel. Every kid deserves it.
By the Numbers

Raised for Alameda County Food Bank at age 9
20%
Of business profits donated to Samaritan House

Community orgs he regularly volunteers for
Lennon
Fells
Founder, Fuel Their Future
The Origin

A Visionary From the Start

Lennon Fells is a freshman at Sacred Heart Schools, Atherton and a member of the Young Men's Service League (YMSL) Peninsula chapter. But his story of impact started long before high school.

At just 9 years old, after learning about food insecurity for the first time, Lennon didn't wait for someone else to act. He launched a fundraising campaign and raised $13,000 for the Alameda County Food Bank — proving that the size of your impact has nothing to do with the size of your age.

"What began as a donation campaign — delivering food and drinks to the local Boys & Girls Club — grew into a movement. Fuel Their Future is built on the belief that student-athletes should lead the conversation about food access, not just benefit from it."

The Entrepreneur

Building With Purpose

In 4th grade, Lennon launched Lennon's Sweetpop — a dessert-flavored popcorn business with a built-in social mission. From day one, he committed 20% of all profits to the Samaritan House, a local organization providing under-served families with essential resources. Even as a young entrepreneur, Lennon understood that business and service aren't opposites — they're partners.

Hands-On Service

Seeing the Need Firsthand

Through YMSL, Lennon volunteers across a range of philanthropies — but his focus has always gravitated toward programs fighting food insecurity. He doesn't just give from a distance. He shows up.

  • Self Help for the Elderly (San Mateo) — Regularly delivers fresh and frozen lunches to elderly and underserved residents.
  • Hillsborough Harvest Garden — Volunteers at the community garden that donates fresh produce directly to the Samaritan House.
  • Boys & Girls Club Peninsula, East Palo Alto — Assists with Second Harvest Food Bank grocery and produce box distribution, putting food directly in the hands of families who need it.

This direct, on-the-ground work has shaped Lennon's understanding of food insecurity in a way no classroom could. He sees the need. He feels the urgency. And that's exactly why he built Fuel Their Future.

The Athlete

Competing at Two Sports

Lennon competes in football and basketball, and knows firsthand how vital proper nutrition is for performance, recovery, and longevity as an athlete.

🏈
Football
🏀
Basketball

Inspired by his father and uncle — gifted athletes who grew up with limited means — Lennon is committed to helping young athletes like them who lack access to the nutrition they need to succeed. He's heard their stories about competing in Boys & Girls Club leagues because those programs offered free athletics to kids whose families couldn't afford club or rec sports.

From Inspiration to Action

After spending time watching youth basketball at the Boys & Girls Club Peninsula, Lennon was moved by what he saw — talented young athletes competing hard, without the nutritional foundation they needed to truly perform. Fuel Their Future was built to change that. The organization collects and donates food and drink items that provide the carbohydrates and protein athletes need before games and practices, along with the protein and electrolytes essential for muscle recovery afterward.

The Vision

Student-Athletes Leading the Way

Lennon's organizing belief — the idea at the heart of Fuel Their Future — is that student-athletes shouldn't just be recipients of food equity efforts. They should be leading them. They understand the connection between nutrition and performance better than anyone. And when they use that understanding to serve their communities, they become the kind of leaders who make the world better both on and off the field.

That's the mission. That's Lennon's story.

Daniel
Fells
10 NFL Seasons • 2015 Ed Block Courage Award Winner
The Player

A Decade at the Highest Level

Daniel Fells spent 10 seasons competing as a Tight End in the NFL — the most competitive athletic arena in the world. Across seven teams, he earned his place on every roster through relentless preparation, physical resilience, and an uncompromising commitment to his craft.

A decade in the NFL teaches you something no playbook can — what it takes to perform when the stakes are real, the pressure is maximum, and everyone is watching. That's the message Daniel carries into every room he enters today.

NFL Career

Seven Teams. One Standard.

Atlanta Falcons '06–07
Oakland Raiders '07
Tampa Bay Buccaneers '08
St. Louis Rams '08–10
Denver Broncos '11
New England Patriots '12–13
New York Giants '14–15

Playing for seven different franchises — each with a different system, coaching staff, and culture — gave Daniel a perspective on adaptability, trust, and performance that few people in the world possess. He didn't just survive the transitions. He thrived through them.

"A father and son building something together — to serve other people's children. That's the story. That's the mission."

The Mission

From the Field to the Community

Today, Daniel channels that decade of NFL experience into something bigger than football. As Lead Speaker and NFL Ambassador for Fuel Their Future, he brings elite-level credibility, personal resilience, and authentic community voice to every speaking engagement, school assembly, and corporate event he delivers.

He connects the dots between what athletes eat, how they perform, and why every child — regardless of their zip code — deserves access to the nutrition that makes performance possible. It's not just a message. It's his life's experience, on every stage.

Speaking Topics
For Schools & Student-Athletes
"Focus, Trust, Finish" — The performance mindset that took me from the practice field to the NFL — and how every student-athlete can build it. Includes nutrition, resilience, and community responsibility.
For Sports Teams & Organizations
"What It Takes" — The inside story of competing across 7 NFL teams. Building winning culture, mental preparation, elite nutrition, and what separates good athletes from great ones.
For Corporate Audiences
"Building a Winning Culture" — What the NFL taught me about accountability, trust, and what separates good teams from great ones. Purpose-driven leadership with a performance edge.
